Position you looking for?: Hello. I am Peter from South Korea. I want you to help me find a job. I do not care no matter what kind of job I would have a role in as long as the employer can deal with my work permit.  And I am willing to pay for the recruiting service fees as well.
Describe yourself:
1.Self-introduction


I would like to share my childhood with you and who I am. 
When I was the baby of the family, I swear I was not spoiled. I know there are many spoiled brats who do not behave well because his or her parents have given him or her everything that he or she wanted.
However, I normally reused almost everything owned by my older sister because she had control of having a thing. Even, I wore her used clothes without my own room until my 20's. Actually, my family lived in financial hardship. We are considered to be a lower class in society. However, even though we live in a difficult situation, our family makes an effort to be positive-minded and be happy. I have always been proud of my parents as they live an honest life and they are kind to those who are even more unfortunate than us. 

In the meantime, I have always been curious and interested in people since childhood.  Every time we’d make a trip into the city, I was not interested in shopping or eating delicious food in the city but of people-watching. As I watched people in a busy city, I would often have thoughts such as, “I wonder what they are thinking,” “If I approach them, what would they think?” “If they were wearing some other style of clothing and walking next to someone else, how would they look?”, or “How can I help this person?”

For the last 10 years as a member of the Red Cross, I have been making monthly monetary donations even though it has only been small amounts. This past year, on weekend, I have spent time with unfortunate people living on the streets by providing coffee, snacks, or warm water along with conversations due to teachings from my parents since childhood. I learned quickly that these people did not care about the price of the food that I provided for them but whether my intentions were sincere. I felt that they were in need of warm-hearted understanding and be treated with dignity as a human being. I believe that one should be kind to the weak. 


 
2.The reason why I want to get a job abroad

In fact, there are three reasons why I want to get a job abroad.

First, I believe that there is nothing like applying for a job overseas to find my potential and go beyond the framework of my existing ideas. Because if I work and communicate with people with different perspectives and ideas in a broader world and embrace diversity and broaden my view of perspectives, I think I can at some point improve my wrong misconceptions, wake up my potential and overcome limitations.

The second is due to my high interest in overseas local business development and investment opportunities. I am currently exporting and sourcing products related to Korean products in Korea. However, if I get a job overseas, I will be able to sell products, services, and assets owned by foreign companies to Korea, Japan, and China, or conversely, to source some products, services, and assets from Asian countries.

When I do sales and sourcing for a local company abroad, I think I will naturally get distribution and network information, which I think will be a different level of information than when I am just doing business in Korea. In the end, working on the various overseas business industry will inevitably lead to new business development and investment opportunities associated with Asian countries and others.

Thirdly, it is because of the education of future children. In fact, Korea is the world's hottest and most competitive education country. So, even young infants go to private institutes and study until late in the evening. But contrary to the passion for education, it's an ironic country that hasn't produced even one Nobel laureate so far.
I think the main reason here is fierce competition. Learning something is because you need to have curiosity and fun and that the depth of learning is increased through communication and discussion rather than competition.
At present, Korean education is mostly the same type of memorization education as it used to be in the 1960s, and it has been ranked and competed through numerous school tests since childhood. The nature of education soon became a tool for college entrance examinations. Therefore, I hope at least my children will be able to collaborate with children of different nationalities, to engage in diversity, to foster creativity, and not to engage in intensely competitive education in Korea.


3.My strengths


Here are some of my strengths.


The first, I am a human type who is willing to take risks and challenge when I make a decision. In the past, I went to England twice to meet a woman whom I fell in love with at first sight.


Second, I am a human type who cares about others. I normally respect the elders and try to make my subordinates feel comfortable acquiring manners through the good Confucian culture that is called “ Yoo-Kyo in Korean. 


Thirdly, I am a kind of human type who must fulfill a certain schedule at a promised time.  I try to do my best to finish my work at the company. When I finish work, I eat dinner, exercise for about two hours, and before I go to bed, I finish my day by reading books. Also, on weekends, rather than wasting time at home, I have a habit of going to public libraries to make business plans and reading books.


Fourth, I am a human type who is always eager to learn about new fields. I have certificates in various fields such as trade and tax accounting.
Currently, I am studying French as my second foreign language.
